description: This lecture discusses the proofs of consistency and liveness properties in longest chain consensus, focusing on the common prefix property. It emphasizes the importance of a balanced leader sequence and the implications of Byzantine nodes on the consensus process. The proof relies on several assumptions, including the integrity of the Genesis block and instant communication among honest nodes.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the common prefix property in longest chain consensus, detailing the necessary conditions for consistency and liveness. It explains how a balanced leader sequence is crucial for achieving these properties and outlines the assumptions needed for the proof.
